obligations.reason: The scope exposes external JTAG pins, debug_mode_i, dmi_jtag/SimJTAG/SimDTM instances, debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, and debug bus signals; verify debug presence is not a bypass and that authorization is actually used at the RTL use sites.
obligations.subject: JTAG/DTM/debug requester
obligations.object: debug module accessible registers, hart debug request/reset controls, and any downstream protected memory/peripheral reachable via dm_slave or dm_master
obligations.expected_guard: debug authentication/authorization such as jtag_unlock or equivalent lifecycle/debug enable must mediate any debug/JTAG operation that can read/write protected state or assert hart debug/reset controls
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DTM inputs -> dmi_jtag/SimJTAG/SimDTM -> debug request/response signals -> debug module slave/master interfaces
state_conditions_to_check: testmode_i or simulation-only path enable conditions
required_evidence_slots: subject: external JTAG or simulation debug requester
operation: DMI read/write or debug reset/request
object: debug registers/hart controls/downstream bus target
expected guard: jtag_unlock or debug authorization/lifecycle check
observed RTL guard or missing guard at request generation/use site
path/reachability from ports/instances to object
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Only top-level signals and instances are visible in the fact layer; inspect connections and conditionals around the debug instances to determine actual mediation.

obligations.reason: The fact layer shows acc_ctrl, reglk_ctrl, priv_lvl_i, acc_ctrl_c indexing, and many NoC peripheral endpoints; verify that all visible endpoint request paths use the access policy consistently rather than only selected paths such as rom_req.
obligations.subject: CPU/NoC requester with privilege level
obligations.operation: configure access policy and route/read/write peripheral requests
obligations.object: peripheral access-control policy acc_ctrl and protected peripheral request enables
obligations.expected_guard: each protected peripheral request should be gated by the access-control matrix entry for the current requester privilege, and writes to the policy should be controlled by lock/privilege mediation
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl -> acc_ctrl_c privilege-indexed gates -> peripheral request enables for bootrom/CLINT/PLIC/crypto/debug/reset/acct/reglk/DMA
state_conditions_to_check: updates from acct_wrapper via AXI and we_flag
special alias expression for j==5 using acc_ctrl[4*4+i]
priv_lvl_i encoding and out-of-range or debug-mode behavior

operation: peripheral access or policy configuration
object: acc_ctrl entries and endpoint request enables
expected guard: acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] and lock/privilege for updates
observed guards on each endpoint path

obligations.reason: reglk_wrapper has AXI access, jtag_unlock input, reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, reglk_ctrl_o and reset input rst_9; check whether lock bits can be modified, cleared, or bypassed after being set.
obligations.subject: AXI software requester and JTAG unlock path
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl lock bits controlling peripheral/register write protection
obligations.expected_guard: lock registers should be writable only under allowed conditions, should become sticky or only clear under authorized reset/unlock, and should guard all protected write paths at use sites
obligations.candidate_path: AXI writes -> reglk_wrapper -> reglk_ctrl_o -> protected wrappers/peripherals
state_conditions_to_check: whether set lock bits can transition back to unlocked
whether reglk_ctrl_i protects writes to reglk itself
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/software requester or JTAG unlock requester
operation: write to reglk control register
object: reglk_ctrl/reglk_ctrl_o bits
expected guard: existing lock state, acct_ctrl_i, privilege/access control, authenticated jtag_unlock for unlock
observed write enable/address decode/data update behavior
reset/clear path evidence
use-site evidence in downstream wrappers
obligations.uncertainty: Need source inspection around reglk_wrapper line ~86 and top-level jtag_unlock generation to determine stickiness and mediation.

obligations.reason: acct_wrapper exposes AXI read/write signals and acc_ctrl_o, with reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i inputs; verify policy configuration cannot be rewritten or loosened without required lock/accounting guard.
obligations.subject: AXI software requester
obligations.expected_guard: access-control policy registers should only be changed by authorized requester and protected by reglk_ctrl/acct_ctrl; updates should not occur when locked or from unauthorized privilege/debug contexts
obligations.candidate_path: AXI writes -> acct_wrapper -> acc_ctrl_o -> acc_ctrl_c -> endpoint access decisions
state_conditions_to_check: whether reads expose policy state acceptably
whether reglk_ctrl_i is checked on all update paths

obligations.reason: rst_wrapper accepts AXI, reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, debug_mode_i and emits many peripheral resets; malicious reset can clear locks, policy, or crypto state unless guarded.

obligations.expected_guard: software-triggered resets of security-sensitive peripherals should require access-control/lock mediation and debug-specific authorization where debug_mode_i is involved

obligations.reason: CLINT controls timer_irq_o and ipi_o and has generic AXI-lite request decoding; confirm upstream access control is preserved and writes are not accepted solely by address handshake from unauthorized subjects.

obligations.object: CLINT timer/IPI control registers accessed through axi_lite_interface
obligations.expected_guard: writes to timer compare/software interrupt or other CLINT control state should be allowed only for authorized privilege/access-control context or protected by upstream peripheral gate
obligations.candidate_path: CLINT AXI-lite interface -> address_o/en_o/we_o/data_o -> timer/software-interrupt registers
state_conditions_to_check: whether invalid addresses still produce writes or data exposure
